channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Achieving high-performance room-temperature organic ferromagnetic semiconductor films via topochemical reduction
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 15 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71866-2

Developing high-performance organic ferromagnetic semiconductors is hindered by the coupling between radical generation and molecular assembly during synthesis. Here, the authors use topochemical reduction to enable radical generation in pre-ordered perylene diimide films, maintaining their long-range order and achieving room temperature ferromagnetism and semiconducting behaviour
item.dc:title: Achieving high-performance room-temperature organic ferromagnetic semiconductor films via topochemical reduction
item.dc:creator: Yanuo Zhu
Qinglin Jiang
Hanlin Gan
Jiaji Yang
Xiandong He
Wei Cui
Shaohua Tong
Liang Yao
Jiang Zhang
Yuguang Ma

item.title: Breakthrough energy storage in medium-entropy superparaelectrics by triple synergy strategy
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 15 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71894-y

Authors propose a triple-synergy strategy for dielectric ceramics that integrates medium-entropy superparaelectric design, local structural distortion and plate-like BiTO phase construction, thereby achieving remarkable energy storage performance.
item.dc:title: Breakthrough energy storage in medium-entropy superparaelectrics by triple synergy strategy
item.dc:creator: Yang Liu
Jiarui Wang
Lulu Liu
Jigong Hao
Peng Li
Zhongbin Pan
Jianli Wang
Min Zhang
Limei Zheng
Hairui Bai
Song Chen
Wei Li
Zhenxiang Cheng

item.title: Dipolar interaction-mediated molecular anchoring electrolyte enables wide-temperature sodium-ion batteries with enhanced safety and durability
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 15 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71861-7

Different challenges in safety, lifespan and temperature adaptability with synergistic interfacial compatibility hinder the development of sodium batteries. Here, this work presents a dipolar interaction-mediated molecular anchoring electrolyte to construct robust interphases for durable wide-temperature (−60 ~ 70 °C) sodium batteries.
item.dc:title: Dipolar interaction-mediated molecular anchoring electrolyte enables wide-temperature sodium-ion batteries with enhanced safety and durability
item.dc:creator: Yong-Li Heng
Zhen-Yi Gu
Yuan-Zheng Tang
Han-Hao Liu
Xiao-Tong Wang
Jie Wang
Shu-Yu Li
Xing-Long Wu

item.title: Supramolecular engineering of amyloid-inspired tripeptide assemblies enabling tunable piezoelectricity
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 15 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71734-z

Peptide assemblies can be used to prepare piezoelectric materials, but the role of peptide sequence and side chains is not well understood. Here, the authors report the study of the factors on the supramolecular stacking and piezoelectric properties of diphenylalanine inspired tripeptide assemblies.
item.dc:title: Supramolecular engineering of amyloid-inspired tripeptide assemblies enabling tunable piezoelectricity
item.dc:creator: Yehong Huo
Shuaijie Liu
Bingbing Yang
Jian Hu
Lingling Li
Xiangnan Gong
Mei-Ling Tan
Peng Liu
Kaiyong Cai
Wei Ji

item.title: Octahedral-rigidity-engineered linear dielectrics for harsh-temperature energy storage capacitors
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 15 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71800-6

The authors engineer an octahedrally rigid perovskite dielectric with a dual-cubic framework and cold-sintering-enabled microstructure, enabling stable capacitive energy storage up to 270 °C.
item.dc:title: Octahedral-rigidity-engineered linear dielectrics for harsh-temperature energy storage capacitors
item.dc:creator: Qi Wang
Qiuyu Zheng
Xuetong Zhao
Jing Guo
Shenglin Kang
Jianglin Wang
Yuchen Li
Xinyuan Hou
Hongwen Liu
Li Cheng
